methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $80M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
DZYNE Technologies is a Growth-stage defense and security company founded by Matt McCue that has raised $80M+. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 10 private and 2 public peers in the Defense & Security sector, including Anduril Industries, Shield AI, and Scale AI, though narrow stage-matched comparables are limited.

Stage-progression analysis, the primary applicable method, uses total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.2x multiplier typical for Growth-stage advancement, yielding $96M. Peer-median valuation was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the Defense & Security vertical; while 21 same-sector peers exist broadly, fewer than three met tight filtering criteria, making a sector-wide median statistically misleading.

The triangulated valuation range is $82M to $110M, with a median point of $96M. This single-method triangulation carries material uncertainty given reliance on stage progression alone. Key variables affecting ultimate valuation include concrete revenue and growth metrics (not provided), capital efficiency relative to disclosed peer multiples, and defensibility of technology within the crowded frontier-defense landscape. The $80M+ raised anchor should be stress-tested against revenue generation and customer concentration before finalizing a valuation commitment.
